title: TC001-Open Focus Mode Drawer and verify core UI is visible
description: Verifies the user can open the Focus Mode Drawer from the dashboard and see key focus controls and stats.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3002/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3002/")
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (explicit navigation per test step) to reach the login page because the current page is stuck on a loading state and has no interactive elements.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3002/login")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/' in current_url
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Focus Drawer')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Focus Drawer' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Start Focus')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Start Focus' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Loading spinner present and prevented access to the application's interactive UI after multiple attempts to load.
- 0 interactive elements detected on the page, so login and dashboard controls were not accessible.
- Navigation to /login did not reveal the login form or any input fields required for authentication.
- Focus Drawer could not be tested because the application UI did not render and the drawer/open button was not available.

title: TC002-Adjust focus duration with plus and minus controls while Ready

TEST FAILURE

title: TC010-Successful login redirects to dashboard

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

    browser = None
    context = None

        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability

async def run_test():

                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability

        )

        # Open a new page in the browser context

        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Session finished')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Session finished' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
